observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO follow the procedural guidelines laid out in Item 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the reconciliation of their records; Item 7 for the disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs; and Item 8 for the procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability. 

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, after all the procedural guidelines are conducted, disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the required disclosures enumerated in Item 9 of COA Circular No. 2020-006.
observations.reason: The reconciliation of the PPE records between the accounting office and the GSO is still ongoing as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant identify/trace the noted unreconciled variance between the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, and adjust the books accordingly; henceforth, to periodically reconcile the reciprocal accounts.
observations.reason: Accounting records traced were for CY 2020-2024 only, transactions before those date are still pending for reconciliation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Exhaust all efforts in identifying the unliquidated cash advances prior to CY 2020; and

Conduct periodic reconciliation with the Municipal Treasurer to avoid the accumulation of unliquidated cash advances; and

Additionally, we recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:

Immediately submit liquidation reports for the cash advances which are still unliquidated in the Accountant’s books.
observations.reason:
Ongoing identification of the detailed unliquidated cash advances.

No mechanism in place for the periodic reconciliation of their records.

Not all liquidation reports were submitted to the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the LDRRMO, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Budget Officer reconcile their records. We likewise recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer prepare separate RAAOs for the QRF, MOOE, and CO of the LDRRM Fund.
observations.reason: Separate RAAOs were not prepared.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Finance Committee, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, ensures the preparation and submission of quarterly reports on fund utilization and the status of program/project implementation using the prescribed format (Annex C) of DBM Local Budget Circular No. 151 dated March 30, 2023, for the LGSF-SBDP 2023 and in succeeding years, if still applicable.
observations.reason: Reports were not received by the Audit Team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Accountant to: 

determine the details of the fund transfers; and

maintain subsidiary ledgers for each fund transfer received;

We likewise recommended that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to facilitate the return of the unutilized fund transfers to the source agencies.
observations.reason:
Details of the fund transfers were unidentifiable.

No subsidiary ledgers were maintained.

Unutilized fund transfers were not yet returned to source agencies.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the: 

Municipal Budget Officer in the preparation of annual and supplemental budgets:

use the correct amount of Past Year Actual Receipts and Expenditures as bases for the Budget Year Proposed Receipts and Expenditures; 

limit the allocation for Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses (EME) to the Office of the Municipal Mayor; and

comply with the 2% statutory limitation for EME based on the actual collections of real property taxes in the next preceding calendar year in compliance with Section 325(h) of RA No. 7160.

Municipal Accountant:

strengthen control on the disbursement of funds by disapproving payments charged against the EME in excess of the 2% limitation; and

submit the journal entry vouchers and liquidation reports of the charges against EME without details provided in the journal.
observations.reason:
Management used incorrect bases for the proposed receipts and expenditures.


Per verification of the CY 2024 Annual Budget, the Office of the Municipal Treasurer had an allocation for EME.

Management did not comply.



No strong internal control is in place for the charging of expenses against the EME.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

Municipal Assessor to submit to the Municipal Treasurer the Assessment Rolls on or before December 31 of each year, in accordance with Section 248 of the Local Government Code of 1991.

Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant, at the beginning of each year, a duly certified list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year based on the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Taxpayer’s Index Card.

OIC - Municipal Accountant to set up the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivables based on the list submitted by the Municipal Treasurer, in compliance with the Modified Accrual method of accounting for income from real property tax.
observations.reason:
Municipal Treasurer did not furnish the OIC-Municipal Accountant the list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible.




The OIC-Municipal Accountant was not able to set up the receivables because no related reports were submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Require the RCC for the immediate restitution of her cash shortage amounting to ₱1,046,926.66; 

Withhold the payment of salary and other emoluments except for terminal leave benefits or gratuities, due to the RCC.  Henceforth, ensure that all collections are remitted and deposited daily and intact.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to strengthen internal control over the issuance of accountable forms to RCCs to prevent similar circumstances in the future.
observations.reason: Cash examination report with cash shortage was already submitted to higher authorities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to submit immediately all the lacking DVs and its supporting documents to the Audit Team for purposes of review/audit.  Henceforth, submit DVs, transaction documents, and financial reports within the reglementary period, pursuant to the pertinent provisions of PD No. 1445 and the RSSA.
observations.reason: Disbursement vouchers with an aggregate amount of ₱6,073,690.50 remained unsubmitted as of year-end. However, a Notice of Suspension was already issued for these unsubmitted DVs..

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive create an Inventory, Appraisal and Disposal Committee to take charge the sorting of the unserviceable assets, appraisal of valuable assets and disposal or divestment of the properties through destruction/condemnation, public auction, sale, barter, or transfer to other government agencies.
observations.reason: No disposal procedure was conducted by the LGU. This was a reiterated audit finding.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit Fund Receipt and Utilization Reports (FRUR) for the 20 % DF within the period prescribed in Section 163 of GAM for LGUs Volume I, to enable reconciliation of expenditure items with the records of the Municipal Budget Officer, Office of the Municipal Engineer, Office of the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator, and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC).
observations.reason: No submission of FRUR for the 20% Development Fund. This is a reiterated audit finding.

observations.recommendation: Immediately submit the lacking supporting documents. This is without prejudice to any disallowance that may be issued, of warranted. Stop the practice of paying without complete supporting documents.
observations.reason: Lacking supporting documents were not submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Local Chief Executive to require the explanation of the shortages established during the physical count and the immediate return of the properties not found during such count and to prevent the losses, damages, or deterioration of properties by exercising due diligence of a good father of a family in supervising accountable officers under his control by ensuring the proper custody of properties and equipment.
observations.reason: No explanation was rendered by the management.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO follow the procedural guidelines laid out in Item 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the reconciliation of their records, and initiate the disposal of unserviceable properties. We likewise recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant revisit the CIP Accounts and make necessary adjusting entries, if applicable.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant identify/trace the noted unreconciled variance between the accounts Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds, and adjust the books accordingly; henceforth, to periodically reconcile the reciprocal accounts.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant trace the records and supporting documents of the fund transfers which were unidentifiable as to its source, and prepare subsidiary ledgers.  We also recommended to return any unutilized balances to donor agencies.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant review the Loans Payable, coordinate with the bank to identify the difference, and prepare adjusting entries, if necessary. We also recommended to correctly present the Current and Non-current portion of the Loans Payable, and provide sufficient disclosure in the accompanying Notes to Financial Statements as required in IPSAS 1.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
promptly submit liquidation reports for the remaining unliquidated amounts;
ensure that future cash advances will be liquidated in accordance with Item 5.1 of COA Circular No. 97-002; and
regularly reconcile records with the OIC-Municipal Accountant.
We likewise recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant monitor regularly the grant and liquidation of cash advances to ensure that no employee will be granted with additional cash advance if the previous one remains unliquidated.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a.)	Municipal Budget Officer and GAD Focal Person to enhance their coordination in the charging of disbursements to the GAD Fund;
b.)	Management shall refrain from charging to the Fund expenditures that are not included in the GAD Plan and Budget; and
c.)	Management to ensure that expenditures are aligned with the activities outlined in the approved GAD Plan and Budget and within the approved appropriation.

We recommended that the LDRRM Council headed by the Municipal Mayor evaluate and review the LDRRMF Investment Plan with emphasis on the proper identification and selection of specific priority PPAs to accelerate their implementation and achieve the 100% utilization of the Municipality’s LDRRM – Mitigation Fund.

We recommended that the LDRRMO, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Budget Officer reconcile their records. We likewise recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer prepare separate RAAOs for the QRF, MOOE, and CO of the LDRRM Fund.

We recommended that the LCE direct the:
a. Municipal Accountant to maintain separate subsidiary ledger for each beneficiary barangay and project(s) under the LGSF-SBDP; and
b. Local Finance Committee, in coordination with the Municipal Accountant, to ensure the preparation and submission of quarterly reports on fund utilization and status of program/project implementation using the prescribed format (Annex C) of the aforementioned Local Budget Circulars.

We recommended that the OIC-Municipal Accountant prepare and submit Fund Receipt and Utilization Reports (FRURs) for the 20% Development Fund to enable reconciliation of expenditure items with the records of the Municipal Budget Officer and Office of the Municipal Engineer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure that all the accountable officers of the LGU are duly bonded. We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer make sure that the collections of his RCOs do not exceed their maximum cash accountability, otherwise, adjust their bonds, to cover their maximum collections.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize in the books the cost of the donated ambulance and its accompanying depreciation. Henceforth, ensure that any donated properties are recorded as soon as they are received. 

We further recommended that the Municipal GSO include the cost of the donated ambulance in the RPCPPE.

We recommended that the:

Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO:

trace all records available for the Trust Fund Inventories still carried in the books since 2019, and prepare adjusting journal entries, if applicable; and

henceforth, to regularly reconcile their Inventory records;

Municipal GSO: 

submit to the Municipal Accountant the SSMI for the week covering the issuance to the end-user of the Welfare Goods for Distribution, copy furnish the Audit Team; and 

moving forward, ensure that SSMI is prepared weekly and submitted to the Municipal Accountant; 

Municipal Accountant:

submit to the Audit Team the distribution list of the Welfare Goods for Distribution worth ₱2,506,575.00;

prepare the journal entry for the issuance to end-user of the Welfare Goods for Distribution once the SSMI from the Municipal GSO is received; and

record in the books the Fuel, Oil, and Lubricants Inventory worth ₱5,690.00 still on hand as of year-end.
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary adjusting entries.

We recommended that the management utilize the LDRRMF strictly for the implementation of projects and activities exclusively for disaster prevention, mitigation, preparedness, response, rehabilitation, and recovery.

We recommended that the Local Development Council and all other offices involved ensure that all procurements are meticulously and judiciously planned.

We also recommended that the MPDC and Municipal Engineer, through proper coordination with other offices involved, ensure the timely implementation of infrastructure projects.

We recommended that Management:

Observe strictly the existing rules and regulations on payment of claims; and,

Not to pay on any claim that is not supported with appropriate and complete documents.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that the:

LCE, in coordination with the MBO, makes realistic or doable targets in the preparation of the Annual Budget by consulting all concerned officials and employees during such preparations;

Sangguniang Bayan review appropriately the Budget before approving; and,

Municipal Treasurer adopts more effective collection strategies and intensifies collection campaigns to meet the collections targets every year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, through the Municipal Planning Development Officer and Municipal Engineer, fast-track and monitor the implementation and completion of the projects in the CY 2022 AIP.  

Henceforth, ensure that PPAs in the AIP are implementation-ready that can be completed during the year to maximize the utilization of the 20% DF and achieve the expected socio-economic and environmental benefits by its constituents.

We recommended that the:

Municipal Accountant demand for the liquidation of the fund transferred to Barangays in previous years or an update regarding the extent of implementation of the program/project/activity; and

Local Chief Executive ensures that the MOA for ensuing fund transfers contains specific provisions for the implementation period and submission of liquidation reports by the implementing units.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive rescinds the individual contracts of services to consider the daily rate instead of monthly payments.

We recommended that the LCE submit justification for approving the payment of transportation allowances to the LGU casual employees, Barangay Tanod and Health Workers and other individuals supported with sufficient documents to establish the validity and propriety of the payments.
We recommended the submission of justification for the deemed irregular procurement and distribution of relief goods.

observations.recommendation:
1.10 In view of the noted lapse, we recommended that Management instruct
the-
• Inventory Committee to conduct annual physical inventory of all
properties and equipment of the LGU and accomplish the
corresponding RPCPPE to be submitted to the Office of the Auditor
within the prescribed period;
• Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer to maintain PPE
Ledger Cards and Property Cards for easy reconciliation of the
account;
• General Services Officer to maintain ARE/PAR and ensure its renewal
every three years; and
• Municipal Accountant to reconcile PPE records with the General
Services Officer and to effect necessary adjusting entries for variances
noted, if any, to establish the accuracy of the reported balances of the
PPE.

2.4 We recommended that LGU Management require the-
• Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the Department of Public Works
and Highways (DPWH) for the determination of the provincial,
municipal and barangay roads within the municipality;
• Inventory Committee to conduct comprehensive annual physical
inspection and count of local road network projects in the
Municipality;
• General Services Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer,
to prepare the Report on the Physical Count of Local Road Network
with complete details as to the location, measurement and amount,
etc., maintain and update Local Road Inventory and Road Map
annually and submit such reports to this Office for proper evaluation.

3.5 In light with the foregoing observations, we recommended that
Management:
• Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer to cause the speedy
liquidation of the unliquidated cash advances and ensure complete
documentation for the expenses charged to the cash advances to
avoid audit suspensions;
• Discontinue granting additional cash advances to the Acting
Municipal Treasurer without liquidation of prior cash advances by
the latter;
• Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure the completeness of
supporting documents in the grant and liquidation of cash advances
before approval of such; and
• Henceforth, adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on
the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances and COA
Circular No. 2012-001 prescribing documentary requirements for
government transactions.

4.6 In view of the noted lapses, we recommended that Management –
• Submit the Certified List of All Real Property Tax Delinquencies and the
List of Tax Delinquent for more than five years, if any, and reconcile the
same with the accounting records; and
• Formulate additional strategies to intensify and facilitate tax
collections. If need be, avail of remedies embodied in Section 174 of
R.A. No. 7160 to ensure collection of taxes due the municipal
government in order to increase the collection efficiency.

5.5 We recommended that Management identify projects, programs and
activities with sufficient details and particulars and ensure that in cases
of lump sum appropriations containing general terms, a separate SB
appropriation ordinance/resolution must always be secured by the Local
Chief Executive to serve as an authority to enter into contract on behalf of
the Municipal Government.

6.4 We recommended that Management assess its existing organizational
structure in order to ensure that a reasonable number of plantilla
personnel will be established, thereby, avoiding the hiring of excessive
number of job-order personnel.

8.4 We recommended that Management coordinate with the HRMO on the
submission of the certified copies of Contracts of Service/Job Order
Contracts, verified Accomplishment Reports and copies of approved
DTRs as justifications on the necessity of the services contracted and the
payments made to job order personnel.

9.12 We recommended that Management submit immediately the January to
December journals, trial balances, financial statements, schedules, and
the 2nd semester CY 2023 financial transactions documents identified in
paragraph 1.7 and withhold salaries of the persons responsible for the
non-submission.

10.13 In light of the foregoing observations, we recommended that
Management instruct concerned officers to cause the immediate
submission of the noted lacking documents to this Office to avoid audit
suspensions, as well as lists of all municipal vehicles and equipment for
verification. Henceforth, discontinue the practice of processing payments
to transactions with incomplete documentations and institute measures
for the processing and approval of transactions as adherence to COA
Circular No. 2012-001 for the documentary requirements for all
government transactions and the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 for
procurements.

11.6 In light of the foregoing observations, we recommended that Management
visit the attached annexes and comply to the submission of the lacking
documents as presented. Henceforth, design and implement controls on
the submission of complete documentation before proceeding to payment
as adherence to COA Circular No. 2012-011 to avoid recurrence of such
observation and suspension of agency transactions.

12.5 In light of the foregoing observation, we recommended that management
require all contractors to provide the date of receipt on the Notice to
Proceed after affixing their signatures to serve as the reckoning date of
the contract and as basis in computing liquidated damages, in cases of
delay and/or non-performance by the contractor.

13.6 In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended that Management
require the Municipal Budget Officer to provide this Office the approved
Annual Investment Plan of the RA 7171 fund for CY 2023 for the evaluation
of the disbursements charged to the fund and, henceforth, instruct
concerned employee to furnish copies of the quarterly fund utilization
reports duly received by the DA/NTA, DBM and DOF as required.

14.6 We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Budget Officer
and the MSWD Officer to consider allocating a separate fund for the
SCPWD Fund for priority PPAs addressing the concerns of the senior
citizens and differently-abled persons of the LGU as adherence to the
above-cited provisions.

15.4 In view of the noted deficiency, we recommended that Management
require the Municipal Budget Officer and the MSWD Officer to consider
allocating separate fund for the LCPC. Henceforth, ensure the full and
proper implementation of the PPAs designed to achieve the desired
objective of the fund.

16.7 In view of the foregoing lapses, we recommended the Management the
following:
• Make a satisfactory explanation on the noted unutilized amounts
under the fund;
• Provide supporting documents, if any, for the disbursements made
without legal appropriations as discussed in paragraph 1.6; and
• Henceforth, identify priority developmental projects with sufficient
details and particulars as to the appropriation and period of
implementation to avoid such observation and to achieve the desired
goals of the designed PPAs.

17.4 We recommended that Management should secure an SB enactment to
utilize the CY 2022 unexpended balance of the 20% Development Fund or
submit the same to this Office to support the said utilizations.

18.5 In view of the foregoing observations, we recommended Management to
require:
• MDRRM Officer to prepare and submit to this Office a detailed
accomplishment Report for the fund indicating the check numbers,
DV numbers, payees, particulars and amounts of charges made
against the fund for proper evaluation;
• All concerned personnel to ensure the full and proper
implementation of PPAs embodied in the approved LDRRMF
Investment Plan especially those under the Mitigation Fund for
disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness programs.

19.5 We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provisions of
R.A. No. 10121 and the related subsequent issuances on LDRRMF
utilization to avoid recurrence of such observation. Henceforth, ensure
that LDRRM Fund shall be strictly utilized for the implementation of DRRM
Programs.

20.4 We recommended that Management instruct the:
• Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to immediately transfer
the unutilized portion of the QRF and DRRM-MOOE for the current year
to a separate depository account of DRRMF in the Trust Fund books;
and
• Municipal Budget Officer and MDRRM Officer to include the unutilized
balance, as well as the amount retained in the Special Trust Fund
account, to the succeeding LDRRMFIP of the LGU as continuing
appropriation for five years.

21.3 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant include the required
disclosures on DRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements to come up
with more informative financial statements.

22.8 In the light of the foregoing observations, we recommended that
Management utilize the HGDG in the selection of PPAs and budget
determination to arrive at a more gender-responsive PPAs and ensure the
full and proper implementation thereof.

23.5 In light of the above observation, we recommended that Management:
• Require applicants to secure BP numbers from GSIS as part of the
recruitment process to avoid such observation;
• Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile
their records, and immediately remit noted discrepancies; and
• Henceforth, strictly comply with the Implementing Rules and
Regulations of Republic Act No. 8291.

24.5 In light of the above observation, we recommended that Management:
• Require applicants to secure Membership ID (NID) numbers from
HDMF as part of the recruitment process to avoid such observation;
• Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to
reconcile their records, and immediately remit noted discrepancies;
and
• Henceforth, strictly comply with the Implementing Rules and
Regulations of Republic Act No. 9679.

25.5 In light of the above observation, we recommended that Management:
• Require applicants to secure PhilHealth Identification Number (PIN)
from PHIC as part of the recruitment process to avoid such
observation;
• Instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to
reconcile their records, and immediately remit noted discrepancies;
and
• Henceforth, strictly comply with the Implementing Rules and
Regulations of Republic Act No. 10606.

26.5 We recommended that Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer
reconcile their records on taxes withheld and remitted, immediately remit
to the Bureau of Internal Revenue all unremitted taxes and adjust
accordingly in the books the results of reconciliations. Henceforth, strictly
comply with NIRC provisions to avoid penalties and/or sanctions to be
imposed by the collecting agency.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal GSO follow the procedural guidelines laid out in Item 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 in the reconciliation of their records; Item 7 for the disposition procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs; and Item 8 for the procedures in the derecognition of non-existing/missing PPEs without available record of accountability.

Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, after all the procedural guidelines are conducted, disclose in the Notes to the Financial Statements the required disclosures enumerated in Item 9 of COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant recognize in the books the cost of the donated ambulance and its accompanying depreciation. Henceforth, ensure that any donated properties are recorded as soon as they are received.

We further recommended that the Municipal GSO include the cost of the donated ambulance in the RPCPPE.

We recommended that the:

13.3 Upon scrutiny of the submitted contract documents by the LGU, the audit team
noted the following deficiencies:
a) Non-preparation/submission of APP and its supporting PPMPs contrary to
Section 373 of RA 7160 and Section 7.3.2 of the IRR of RA 9184.

13.4 Section 373 of R.A. 7160 states that “On or before the fifteenth (15th) day of
July each year, the local chief executive shall prepare an annual procurement
program for the ensuing fiscal year which shall contain an itemized list of the
estimated quantity of supplies needed for such year, a complete description
thereof as to kind, quality, estimated cost, and balance on hand”

13.5 Section 7.3.2 of the IRR of Ra 9184 provides:
In the preparation of the indicative APP, the end-user or implementing units of
the Procuring Entity shall formulate their respective Project Procurement
Management Plans (PPMPs) for their different programs, activities, and
projects (PAPs). The PPMP shall include:
a) Information on whether PPAs will be contracted out, implemented by
administration in accordance with the guidelines issued by the GPPB, or
consigned;
b) The type and objective of contract to be employed;
c) The extend/size of contract scopes/packages;
d) The procurement methods to be adopted, and indicating if the procurement
tasks are to be outsourced as provided in Section 7.3.3 of this IRR;
e) The time schedule for each procurement activity and for the contract
implementation; and
f) The estimated budget for the general components of the contract

13.6 The APP shall be approved by the Head of the Procuring Entity and must be
consistent with its duly approved annual budget. Section 7 (2) of the
Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 requires government offices
to prepare, maintain and update an APP for all its procurement, that shall include,
for each individual project, a PPMP.

13.7 Upon review of the contracts entered into by the LGU, the audit team noted that
the approved APP and its supporting PPMPs were not attached to the contract
documents, thus, evaluation whether all procurements were in accordance with
the approved APP of the Procuring Entity could not be done.

13.8 We have recommended that Management ensure that copies of the APP and
its supporting PPMPs be attached to all procurement transactions as basis
that the transactions were included in the approved APP of the agency.

We reiterated our recommendations and the Management agreed that the Municipal Mayor: • instruct the Appraisal Committee to continue prioritizing the valuation of the purchased and donated parcels of land, titled and untitled, which will be used as the basis of the Municipal Accountant in recording the land/adjusting the Land account balance for its fair presentation in the financial statements; and • require the Municipal Assessor to exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of the remaining 16 parcels of land through a Transfer of Certificate of Title (TCT) in the name of the Municipality of Dolores.
observations.managementAction: No action.
observations.reason: Ocular inspection of the parcels of land owned by the Municipality was conducted. Some lots belong to the protected area, hence not likely to be titled in the name of the Municipality. Further, they have conducted ocular inspection of the parcels of land owned by the Municipality and found out that some are not even alienable and disposable. Part of their initiative is through seeking assistance from the Department of Environment and Natural Resources (DENR) and requesting for certifications as to whether the subject lots are alienable and disposable. Moreover, there are lots that are school sites, so the Municipal Mayor mentioned that they will apply for a special patent and eventually, donate the said lots to the Department of Education (DepEd).

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the: • Inventory Committee to coordinate with the Municipal Engineer to use the road network map of the Municipality to facilitate the preparation of a proper inventory and reporting of the Municipality’s road network system; • Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects; • Municipal Treasurer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map and to submit to the Audit Team copies of the complete Local Road Network Property Cards for all roads and its components for validation purposes; and • Municipal Accountant to record the amount of road networks required in the transitory provision of COA Circular No. 2015-008 for the fair presentation of the Property, Plant and Equipment account in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: No action.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower and the voluminous transactions hindered the Municipality to coordinate between the Inventory Committee and the Municipal Engineer. The Municipal Engineer furnished the Audit Team on June 8, 2022 via electronic mail the List of Road Projects from CYs 2010 to 2021, Road Inventory as of December 31, 2021, and summaries of both reports. However, initial verification revealed that the Municipal Engineer’s record do not reconcile with that of the Municipal Treasurer per Report of Local Road Network submitted to the Audit Team on May 31, 2022, as the latter’s record did not include road networks constructed/completed in CY 2021. The Municipal Accountant made adjustments in her records per Journal Entry Voucher No. 100-2022-06-60001 for the transfer of local road networks (2011-2016) from the registry. As for those that are from 2010 and below, she is yet to take-up the same in the books of accounts since they still have to locate the records.

We recommended that the LCE and Legislative Offices expedite the creation of the two (2) mandatory positions for the MDRRMO as well as the hiring process for the three (3) MDRRMO staff responsible for administration and training, research and planning, and operations and warning.
We recommended and the LCE agreed to direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to make necessary adjusting entries to correct the deficiencies above.

We recommended and the LCE agreed to strictly observe the accounting, reporting and full disclosure requirements of Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (LDRRMF) pursuant to Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-12.

We recommended that the LCE, in coordination with the MBO, to make proper planning in the preparation of the Annual Budget in order to avoid unforeseen contingency of all budgetary items and consequently resorting to illegal transfers of savings; and Sangguniang Bayan to adhere strictly to existing rules, laws and regulations in the use of appropriated funds and savings.

We recommended that the LCE, in coordination with the MBO, to make realistic or doable collection targets in the preparation of the Annual Budget by consulting all concerned officials and employees during such preparations; Sangguniang Bayan to review appropriately before approving; and Municipal Treasurer adopt more effective collection strategies and intensify collection campaign to meet the collection targets of every year.

We recommended that management conduct regular and close monitoring of the barangays on the status of compliance with the waste segregation, identify individual problems of each barangay, and formulate strategies to inculcate discipline and encourage participation of the residents in waste segregation.

We recommended and the LCE directs the person(s) involved to strictly adhere to the timely compliance of submission of the documents enumerated in the preceding paragraph pursuant to COA Circular No. 2009-001 and RA 9184. Moreover, as a control of the LGU, we enjoin the LCE to desist from approving the payment of contractual obligations without first ensuring that the contract documents have been submitted and received by the Audit Team.

documents to provide reasonable assurance that the projects were legally
awarded to bidders which are most advantageous to the municipality pursuant

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management:
 instruct the Inventory Committee to conduct the complete annual physical
count of PPE in accordance with Section 124 of the NGAS Manual for
LGUs, Vol. 1. A representative of the Auditor be invited to witness the
inventory taking for this purpose;
 see to it that the Inventory Committee shall prepare the Report on the
Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) which shall
be reconciled with the PPE records kept by the General Services Office
and Accounting Office; and that a copy of the RPCPPE shall be submitted
to COA for verification not later than January 31 of each year pursuant to
Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS.

We have recommended the management that:
 The General Services Officer maintain Property Cards for PPE and
ARE/PAR and ensure that the ARE/PAR be renewed every after three
years pursuant to Section 492 of GAAM, Volume 1, to establish effective
management and control over PPE;
 The Municipal Accountant maintain complete and updated PPELC for
each category of PPE pursuant to Section 114 of NGAS Manual for LGUs
to provide ready information on the accounts and to support balances in
the financial statements.
 The General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant reconcile their
property records with the result of physical count to ensure that PPE
recorded in the property records really exists and that all existing PPE are
recorded in the books, in order to establish correctness and completeness of
balances of PPE accounts. Any variance shall be handled accordingly and
subsequently adjusted as necessary.
 The Property and Accounting Units comply with the prescribed procedures
in COA Circular No. 89-296 on the disposal of unserviceable PPE, if any.

We have recommended the management to require the Inventory Committee
to conduct physical count of its road networks in order to ascertain the
existence of the balance of the account Road Networks. Moreover, direct the
Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant of the complete
description and cost segregation of the road networks to facilitate the
preparation of the subsidiary ledgers for roads and its components. Likewise,
require the General Services Officer to prepare and maintain the Local Road
Network Property Card for all roads and its components, including Road
Map. A Report on Local Road Network is to be prepared by the General
Services Officer at the end of accounting period.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant –
● draw the journal entry voucher to close the CIP accounts to their proper
completed assets accounts as well as the erroneous recording to attain the
correct balances of the accounts;
● provide depreciation for the completed assets;
● submit the lacking supporting documents to validate the completed
projects; and
● submit report of disbursements to liquidate the funds received from NTA
and OCD-DRRM in order to present fairly their respective account
balances in the financial statement.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the journal entry
voucher to bring the correct balances of the accounts. Henceforth, be guided
accordingly with accounting rules and regulations in recording transactions.

voucher to bring the correct balances of the accounts. Henceforth, be guided
accordingly with accounting rules and regulations in recording transactions.

We have recommended the Municipal Accountant see to it that the General
Journals shall be accompanied by Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) to support
the validity and the correctness of accounting entries made.

We have recommended that all Memorandum of Agreements entered into by
the municipality and Notice of Authority to Debit Account Issued be submitted
to the audit team for verification. Henceforth, ensure that funds received from
National Government Agencies and all other sources are covered by
Memorandum of Agreement and that the same shall be submitted to COA
within 5 days from perfection thereof for audit purposes.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant andMunicipal
Treasurer to ensure the submission of accounts and financial statements within
the prescribed periods pursuant to Section 347 of Republic Act No. 7160,
Section 70 of NGAS for LGUs, Item 4 of COA Circular No. 2010-01 and
Paragraph 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We have recommended that management strictly adhere to the rules and
regulations in the submission of all approved contracts within the prescribed
period as provided for in COA Circular No. 2009-001, for a systematic and
effective review process to come up with timely and relevant audit results.

We have recommended that management:
a. Instruct the Project Facilitator to submit a justification for certifying the
project as 100 percent accomplished;
b. require the concerned contractor to employ corrective measures on the
stone masonry and concrete curb and gutter. Otherwise, demand the
refund of the cost of the deficiencies;
c. conduct inspection of the project upon notice of the contractor that said
deficiency was rectified to determine whether corrective measures were
satisfactorily made;
d. submit request for re-inspection of the rectified project to the Auditor for
appropriate action.

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor cause and ensure the
prompt implementation of all projects/programs funded out of the tobacco
excise tax in order to give the constituents, especially the tobacco farmers the
early benefits that could be derived therefrom.

We have recommended that Management submit the required supporting
documents to COA for audit purposes and submit justification for the noted
lapses. Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant see to it that succeeding
transactions be properly supported with complete documentation in
accordance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations in order to establish the
validity and propriety of the transactions and disbursements. Stop the
processing and payment of claims that are not properly documented.

Chairman submit the above-enumerated documentary evidences under
Item 14.4 herein, to COA for audit purposes to support the award of the
contracts and eventually payments thereof. Henceforth, ensure that all
contracts be accompanied by complete documentations required under RA No.
9184 and Its IRR and relevant regulations on procurement.

justification/explanation on the noted violations and ensure that prospective
bidders submit complete eligibility requirements and post the necessary bid
security before they are marked as “passed” and “post-qualified”.

not included in the investment plan.

prepared Annual Procurement Plan (APP) for infrastructure projects, if any,

Fund; and

evaluation of the accomplishments and disbursement of the fund;

2.5 We also have recommended that management undertake the following measures in order to establish, the existence, condition and valuation of local road networks of the LGU:
The Municipal Engineer and the Property Custodian conduct a yearly physical count of local road network projects and consequently update the local road map of the LGU;
The Municipal Engineer provide the Municipal Accountant and the Property Custodian the description and cost segregation of road components of various road infrastructure projects; and
The Municipal Accountant keeps and maintain subsidiary ledger for roads and its components and reconcile the same to the result of the physical count at the end of the year.

3.3 We have recommended that the Municipal Assessor exhaust all possible means to document the legality of ownership of lots through a TCT in the name of LGU.  Also, make representations with the Provincial Assessor’s Office for the proper reconciliation of the municipal and provincial Real Property Records.

We have recommended that the Municipal Treasurer exert extra effort to enforce the collection of the delinquent RPT/SET receivables.  Consider imposing the remedies provided under Section 247 of the Local Government Code.

We have recommended the following:
Reassess the viability of PPAs and fast-track its implementation to warrant delivery of benefits to the intended beneficiaries. Otherwise, return the funds to the respective source agency pursuant to existing laws, rules and regulations;
Return the unexpended balance of completed projects to the respective source agencies; and
Submit to the Audit Team the corresponding liquidation report and other pertinent documents for post-audit.

We have recommended that management determine the cause of delay and address the same in order to comply with the required submission dates of accounts and reports. We have also recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit immediately the overdue accounts and reports for audit and the Municipal Treasurer prepare Report of Checks Issued and Report of Disbursements.

We have recommended that management strictly adhere to the rules and regulations in the submission of all approved contracts within the prescribed period as provided for in COA Circular No. 2009-001 in order to allow the Auditor to conduct timely review, evaluation and prompt detection of any errors/deficiencies.

We have recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Engineer see to it that disbursement vouchers for government contracts/projects are properly supported before payments are made.  Submit the lacking documentations immediately, else, the same shall be suspended/disallowed in audit for absence of sufficient evidence to prove legality and propriety of the transactions.

We have recommended that Management submit the required supporting documents to COA for audit purposes and submit justification for the noted lapses.  Henceforth, the Municipal Accountant see to it that succeeding transactions be properly supported with complete documentation in accordance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations in order to establish the validity and propriety of the transactions and disbursements.  Stop the processing and payment of claims that are not properly documented.

We have recommended the following:
That Management explain/justify why the default mode of procurement which is public bidding was not used in the procurement of goods and services;
That purchases beyond the threshold on shopping and small value procurements be done through public bidding as mandated under R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR; and
That alternative methods of procurement be resorted to only in highly exceptional cases provided for in R.A. No. 9184 and ensure that the same will not constitute splitting of contracts.  In all instances, ensure that the most advantageous price for the LGU is obtained.

12.4.4 We have recommended that management prepare its APP and supporting PPMPs in accordance with the requirements set forth in the Revised IRR of RA 9184 in order to guide management in the procurement of goods and infrastructure projects and furnish COA of the same for audit/evaluation purposes.  Henceforth, no procurement shall be undertaken unless it is in accordance with the approved APP of the procuring entity.

12.5.4 We have recommended that the Technical Working Group (TWG) of the BAC adhere to the prescribed guidelines in the preparation of the ABC.  At all times, ensure that the ABC reflects the most advantageous prevailing price for the government.

12.6.4 We have recommended that the BAC invite observers in writing in all the stages of the procurement process in accordance with the provisions of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

12.7.7 We have recommended that the BAC observe the required pre-procurement conference for infrastructure project with an ABC above ₱5,000,000.00 to ensure that the procurement is in accordance with the project and annual procurement plans and determine the readiness of management for the procurement at hand, among others.

12.8.3 We have recommended that the BAC observe the required pre-bid conference for infrastructure projects with an ABC above ₱1,000,000.00 to ensure that the eligibility requirements and the technical and financial components of the contract to be bid are made clear to all concerned.

12.9.4 We have recommended that the members of the BAC shall initial every page of the original copies of all bids received and opened to ensure the authenticity and integrity of the bid documents.

12.10.4 We have recommended that the members of the BAC ensure that all Bid Securing Declarations submitted are in standard form, if not, require bidders to resort to any other valid form of bid security under Section 27 of the 2016 Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

12.11.4 We have recommended that management ensure that all documentary requirements are complied with prior to entering into contract with the winning bidder.

12.12.3 We have recommended that the BAC Secretariat post copies of the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and approved contract in the PhilGEPS within the prescribed time and submit printout copies to the COA for audit purposes.

12.13.11 We have recommended  that the members of the BAC submit justification/explanation on the noted violations and ensure that prospective bidders submit complete eligibility requirements and post the necessary bid security before they are marked as “passed” and “post-qualified”.

13.5.4 We have recommended that management carefully prepare the 20% Development Fund Investment Plan.  Utilize the fund appropriated for every programs, projects and activities (PPAs) strictly in accordance with the plan and budget to avoid over and under expenditure and partial/non-implementation of PPAs.  Limit expenditures to the amount appropriated for every PPA.

13.6.5 We have recommended that management:
justify the above-noted lapses; and
limit the utilization of the 20% Development Fund to programs, projects and activities that will contribute to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and ensure that the same partakes the nature of investment or capital expenditures that will yield greater benefit to the people in accordance with DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 as updated under DBM-DILG JMC No. 2017-1.

13.8 We have recommended that management:
immediately submit the above-enumerated documents for proper review and evaluation of the said projects funded under the 20% Development Fund; and
adhere with the complete documentary requirements as provided by COA Circular No. 2012-001.  Stop paying claims with incomplete documentation.

13.10  We have  recommended  that the municipality utilize the unexpended balance of allocated funds for the intended purposes after due evaluation whether these are still priority concerns taking into consideration current developments and to accordingly re-appropriate the funds of PPA which have become irrelevant or not responsive to current priority undertakings.

14.4  In view of the noted lapses, we have  recommended the following:
that administrative and office expenses for the LDRRMO be sourced from the general fund and the LDRRMF be strictly utilized for the implementation of DRRM PPAs;
the Municipal Treasurer to transfer only the unutilized portion of QRF and DRRM-MOOE to the separate depository account for LDRRMF and the unutilized CO to remain in the General Fund; and
that a separate LDRRMF Investment Plan be prepared for the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF and should be utilized in accordance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

16.6 We have recommended that management establish their Local Council for the Protection of Children who shall prepare plans and ensure implementation of the same in compliance with the provisions of Section 15 of RA 9344 and DILG MC 2012-120.

If need be, avail of remedies embodied in Section 174 of RA 7160 to ensure collection of taxes due the municipal government in order to increase the delivery of basic services. Further, we also recommended the Municipal Treasurer prepare the Certified List of All Property Tax Delinquencies to include all tax delinquents and reconcile the same with the accounting records.

In view of the foregoing, we recommended that the agency submit the above stated missing documents for evaluation by the team.  Henceforth, be guided with Section 305(a) of RA 7160 and Section 84.1 of PD 1445 before any disbursements of the same nature are made.

We have recommended that all Memorandum of Agreements entered into by the municipality and Notice of Authority to Debit Account Issued be submitted to the audit team for verification.  Henceforth, ensure that funds received from National Government Agencies and all other sources are covered by Memorandum of Agreement and that the same shall be submitted to COA within 5 days from perfection thereof for audit purposes.

We reiterated that the Municipal Treasurer prepare transmittal of submitted vouchers to the Municipal Accountant, who after recording in the Books of Accounts, will then submit the same to the Auditor for the conduct of timely audit and communicate on time deficiencies noted for corrections to improve financial operations of the Municipal Government.  This way, management could easily pinpoint the persons responsible and determine the causes of delay of submission of accounts and reports.

We have recommended management to strictly adhere to the rules and regulations in the submission of all approved contracts within the prescribed period as provided for in COA Circular No. 2009-001 in order to allow the Auditor to conduct timely review, evaluation and prompt detection of any errors/deficiencies.

We have recommended that the agency officials explain/justify the noted lapses especially on the splitting of procurement and direct contracting as method of procurement for the above-mentioned items.  We have also recommended that purchases beyond the threshold on shopping and small value procurements be done through public bidding as mandated under R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR.  Alternative methods of procurement shall be resorted to only in the highly exceptional cases provided for in R.A. No. 9184.  In all instances, the procuring entity shall ensure that the most advantageous price for the LGU is obtained.

We recommended that a special book of account and depository account be maintained solely for the share from Tobacco Excise Tax (RA 7171).

We have recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Sangguniang Bayan Members, thru the issuance of an SB resolution, adapt a comprehensive and integrated solid waste management system in the Municipality in accordance with the provisions of the IRR of R.A. No. 9003 and following the pro-forma ordinance issued by the National Solid Waste Management Commission.

We have recommended that management strictly follow the guidelines laid out under DBM-DILG Joint Circular 2011-1 and include in its 20% Municipal Development Plan only projects that are permanent in nature and shall be fully utilized that yields greater benefit to the people.

In view of the above observations, we have recommended the following:
that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to prepare an investment plan for the LDRRMF of the Municipality and submit the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF using the format in accordance with COA Circular 2012-002, to facilitate the monitoring and evaluation of the accomplishments and disbursement of the fund;
that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the balance of ₱953,797.56 to the separate depository account for LDRRMF; and
that a separate LDRRMF Investment Plan should be prepared for the unutilized balance of the LDRRMF and should be utilized in accordance with pertinent laws, rules and regulations.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive instruct the GAD Focal Point to identify programs, projects and activities that address gender issues such as promotion of gender-responsive governance, protection and fulfillment of women’s human rights and promotion of women’s economic empowerment to be included in the GAD Plan.
We also recommended that the projects and programs under the agency’s GAD Plan be fully implemented.
